tau_b_table_recipe: For each monic cubic b(x)=x^3+p x^2+q x+r in the 50-family catalogue, compute Delta_3=p^2 q^2-4 q^3-4 p^3 r-27 r^2+18 p q r, u=p^2-3q, j_b=256 u^3/Delta_3, then set tau_b to the unique point in the standard fundamental domain F with j(tau_b)=j_b. Equivalently compute roots, transform to Legendre lambda, use tau=i*K(1-lambda)/K(lambda), and reduce by SL_2(Z). Rows 30-33 have u=0 and tau_b=rho=e^{2*pi*i/3}.
trichotomy_substratum_class: TRICHOTOMY_NOT_MODULAR_GEOMETRIC: the j=0 equianharmonic cell is a genuine CM subcell, but the three empirical SIARC bins are not a CM partition, not finite |j| level strata, and not the image of any X_0(N) substratum in X(1).
j=0_cell_predicted_population_at_d=4: 1 in the current Q1/PCF-1-style d=4 catalogue, namely family 32 b=n^4-3n^3-3n^2+3n-3; wider windows may contain more, but no cross-degree A=2d universality is predicted.
j=1728_cell_predicted_population_at_d=4: 0 in the current d=4 catalogue/window. In a much wider quartic enumeration J=0 is a sparse Diophantine condition, so occupancy is not mathematically impossible, but it should not be expected to carry a special universal A=8 rule.
X(1)_plot_recipe: Draw the standard Klein fundamental domain F={tau in H: |tau|>=1, |Re tau|<=1/2}. Compute tau_b by inverse j or AGM periods, reduce to F, color points by SIARC bin (-_S3_CM, +_S3_real, +_C3_real), mark j=0 at rho and j=1728 at i, use special glyphs for rows 30-33, and overlay log(|j|+1) contours to show that the observed drift is a real-analytic coordinate rather than a modular partition.
novel_LMFDB_curves_expected: No genuinely new elliptic curves over Q are expected from the small-coefficient catalogue; after minimal-model reduction most/all should match existing LMFDB isomorphism classes if within the database conductor range. The novel object is the SIARC/PCF annotation of those curves, not new modular geometry.
